benefit: Collateral-free business loans: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,001–₹5L), Tarun (₹5L–₹10L) at subsidised rates. No processing fee under Shishu category.
notes: MUDRA is NOT a direct government disbursement — it is a refinancing scheme. Loans are given by banks/MFIs/NBFCs. Each lender has its own credit assessment. The scheme eligibility sets the minimum bar.

conditions.description: Must be a non-farm micro/small enterprise
conditions.source: MUDRA Guidelines — non-farm income generating activities
conditions.ambiguity_note: 'Allied agricultural' includes poultry, dairy, fisheries, bee-keeping — but pure crop farming is excluded. A farmer with a grain shop qualifies for the shop business, not the farm.

conditions.description: Must NOT be a wilful defaulter

conditions.source: MUDRA Guidelines — wilful defaulters to banks/financial institutions excluded

conditions.description: Business loan requirement must fall within MUDRA limits
conditions.source: MUDRA — maximum loan under Tarun category ₹10 lakh

required_documents.document: Business proof / trade licence / GST registration
Bank account statements (last 6 months, if existing business)
Caste certificate (for priority processing for SC/ST/OBC)
